codeReview.summary: Implementation correctly implements `/exchanges/{id}/volume_chart/range` with canonical `[timestamp, volume_btc]` tuples, ascending ordering, finite numeric validation, and explicit invalid-range validation. Code follows existing project patterns and conventions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation correctly reuses the existing error fixture pattern from `tests/fixtures/error-fixtures.json` (coinChartRangeBadFrom, coinChartRangeBadBounds) for consistent error responses across range endpoints.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tests/invalid-params.test.ts:287-290 - uses errorFixtures.coinChartRangeBadFrom and errorFixtures.coinChartRangeBadBounds for exchange volume range validation errors, matching the pattern established by coin OHLC range endpoints.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly used the `satisfies [number, number]` TypeScript pattern for tuple type safety, consistent with modern TypeScript practices in the codebase.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/modules/exchanges.ts:341 - `.map((row) => [row.timestamp.getTime(), row.volumeBtc] satisfies [number, number])` ensures compile-time tuple shape verification.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the core-api-worker skill procedure correctly: wrote failing tests first, implemented the route, ran targeted tests, and verified with typecheck.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows: 1) initial test run with expected failures (404 before implementation), 2) post-implementation tests passed (87 tests), 3) typecheck passed. skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true with no deviations.

summary: The exchange volume range endpoint is correctly implemented with all required contract semantics: canonical `[timestamp, volume_btc]` tuples, ascending chronological ordering, finite numeric validation, and explicit 400 errors for malformed or reversed `from`/`to` values. Tests cover happy path and invalid parameter cases. The implementation follows existing code patterns and fulfills all four validation contract assertions (VAL-CORE-024 through VAL-CORE-025).
